sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Is Haemonetics (HAE) Leveraging VASCADE MVP XL’s FDA Expansion To Deepen Its EP Moat?
feed.summary: Haemonetics recently received FDA approval to expand the labeling for its VASCADE MVP XL venous vascular closure system, allowing it to be used with larger procedural sheaths in advanced electrophysiology procedures. This expansion, supported by clinical trial data, strengthens Haemonetics' position in the electrophysiology market. While this development offers fresh regulatory support and an improved investment narrative, the article cautions that competitive pressure, reliance on U.S.-centric franchises, and operational challenges remain key concerns for investors.

feed.title: Form FWP CITIGROUP INC Filed by: CITIGROUP INC
feed.summary: Citigroup Global Markets Holdings Inc., guaranteed by Citigroup Inc., has filed a Form FWP for 1.5 Year Dual Directional Buffer Securities linked to the worst performance of the Dow Jones Industrial Average and the Russell 2000 Index. These securities offer a hypothetical payment at maturity based on the underlying returns, with a maximum upside return and a buffer percentage to limit losses. The filing details hypothetical payment scenarios, preliminary terms, and significant risk considerations for potential investors.

feed.title: JPMorgan (AMJB) offers auto-call contingent-interest notes due 2029
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering Auto Callable Contingent Interest Notes due April 5, 2029, with payments linked to the performance of the Nasdaq-100®, Russell 2000®, and S&P 500® indices. These notes offer a Contingent Interest Payment if each index is at or above 70% of its Initial Value, and can be automatically called early if each index meets or exceeds its Initial Value. Investors face credit risk, limited liquidity, and the potential for principal loss if the least performing index falls below its Trigger Value.

feed.title: JPMorgan (AMJB) offers Micron‑linked auto‑call notes with ≥21.75% contingent yield
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering Auto Callable Contingent Interest Notes linked to Micron Technology, Inc. common stock, which price around April 9, 2026, and mature on October 14, 2027. These notes offer a contingent interest rate of at least 21.75% per annum, provided Micron’s stock closes at or above 50% of its initial value on review dates, and may be automatically called as early as October 9, 2026. While offering a high contingent yield, the product carries significant equity downside and issuer credit risk, with potential for principal loss if not called and if the final value is below the trigger value.

feed.title: VICI Properties Inc Stock: Strategic Canadian Expansion and Steady Dividend Growth in Experiential R

feed.title: Super Hi Reports Unaudited Financial Results for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025
feed.summary: Super Hi International Holding Ltd. announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025. The company reported a 10.2% increase in Q4 2025 revenue to US$230.0 million and an 8.0% increase in full-year 2025 revenue to US$840.8 million. Despite strategic investments in employee incentives and customer experiences impacting operating margins, the company saw substantial growth in delivery business and other business segments, and a significant turnaround from a loss to a profit in Q4 2025 and a 69.6% increase in full-year profit.

feed.title: ThinkLabs AI Closes $28 M Series A Led by Energy Impact Partners, Backed by NVentures and Edison International
feed.summary: ThinkLabs AI, a company focused on AI-powered grid intelligence, has successfully closed a $28 million Series A funding round led by Energy Impact Partners, with strong participation from NVentures (NVIDIA’s venture capital arm) and Edison International. This investment highlights the critical need for advanced grid infrastructure as the AI revolution significantly increases electricity demand. ThinkLabs AI's platform uses physics-informed AI to help utilities manage and optimize the electric grid with unprecedented speed and accuracy, addressing challenges like rapid load growth and system capacity limits.
